title: Batch 7 Banknote Metric Stress Validator
targetType: Benchmark comparison or metric validation problem
actualExecutionPerformed: Loaded the public UCI text dataset, ran a deterministic stratified split, trained a most-frequent baseline, a logistic regression challenger, and a shuffled-label negative control, then recorded FP/FN rates.
whatSovrynLearned: The metric validator is useful for comparing a challenger against dummy and shuffled-label controls, but it is explicitly limited because one deterministic split cannot certify an official benchmark protocol or prove absence of leakage.
hardQuestionAnswer: Existing tools were not enough for benchmark comparison or metric validation problem; Sovryn built metric_stress_validator, used it on UCI Banknote Authentication classification dataset, and learned: The metric validator is useful for comparing a challenger against dummy and shuffled-label controls, but it is explicitly limited because one deterministic split cannot certify an official benchmark protocol or prove absence of leakage.
humanReadableSummary: Loaded the public UCI text dataset, ran a deterministic stratified split, trained a most-frequent baseline, a logistic regression challenger, and a shuffled-label negative control, then recorded FP/FN rates. Logistic regression reached accuracy=0.976, balanced_accuracy=0.978, f1_macro=0.976; the shuffled-label control stayed near balanced_accuracy=0.476.
